lede: A look at June 3 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Yelena Isinbayeva and Allen Ginsberg, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Shlomo Argov
events.desc: The Israeli ambassador to the United Kingdom, Shlomo Argov, is shot on a London street; he survives but is left paralysed.

events.title: Maurice Herzog
events.desc: Herzog and Lachenal of the French Annapurna expedition become the first climbers to reach the summit of an 8,000-metre peak.

events.title: Los Angeles
events.desc: In Los Angeles, California, white U.S. Navy sailors and Marines attack Latino youths in the five-day Zoot Suit Riots.

events.title: Franz Rademacher
events.desc: Franz Rademacher proposes plans to make Madagascar the "Jewish homeland", an idea that had first been considered by 19th century journalist Theodor Herzl.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: During the Battle of France, the Luftwaffe bombs Paris.

events.title: Liverpool F.C.
events.desc: Liverpool F.C. is founded by John Houlding.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Battle of Philippi (also called the Philippi Races): Union forces rout Confederate troops in Barbour County, Virginia, now West Virginia.

events.title: Great auk
events.desc: The last pair of great auks is killed.

events.title: Pope Alexander VII
events.desc: Pope Alexander VII appoints François de Laval vicar apostolic in New France.

events.title: Peter Abelard
events.desc: The French scholar Peter Abelard is found guilty of heresy.

deaths.text: Jack Kevorkian, American pathologist, author, and activist
Ruhollah Khomeini, Iranian religious leader and politician, 1st Supreme Leader of Iran
Pope John XXIII, Head of the Catholic Church from 1958 to 1963

observances.text: Christian feast day: Charles Lwanga and Companions (Roman Catholic Church), and its related observances: Martyrs' Day (Uganda)
